setting.note: The Kabul of a boy's memory before the wars: the walled gardens and quiet embassy streets of the affluent Wazir Akbar Khan district, a hill where kites fight over the rooftops each winter, and the lake west of the city where families picnic. The same places return years later scarred by Soviet occupation, civil war and Taliban rule, when a stadium becomes a killing ground.

rights.originalWork: In copyright. Khaled Hosseini, first published 2003. This book is mapped as a cited work: we store none of its text and reproduce no passages. The places are facts and the notes are our own.
rights.textSource: None stored. A cited book reproduces no text.
rights.territoryNotes: Locations and original commentary only. No reproduction of the copyrighted text, in any territory.
rights.attribution: The Kite Runner, Khaled Hosseini (2003). Locations mapped by Read the Places; not affiliated with or endorsed by the author or publisher.

palette.note: The red of a pomegranate and of a fighting kite. A cited book's colour is not a quotation, because we store no text to quote; it still has to be legible, and this clears WCAG AA on paper.
